title: Glassbox updates and C++ fixes
summary:
I spent the last few days working on Glassbox stability. I was having trouble with NaN correlation values. Instead of just replacing them with 0.0, I added a RuntimeWarning so I can actually see what is happening to the predictions.

I also fixed the BFGS optimizer. It was diverging because of Inf values, so now it returns a large finite number instead. I changed the HardConcreteSelector to use straight-through one-hot to match inference time, which helps close the gap between training and testing.

For the C++ side, I cleaned up the test suite. Now I can use pytest to check parity properly. I also fixed the EvolutionaryONNTrainer so all parameters like random_seed and timeout actually get passed down correctly. It should be much more robust now.
commitMessages: fix: improve diagnostics, configurability, and robustness
fix: harden BFGS optimizer, pruning convergence, gate discretization, and BN fusion
fix: resolve critical C++ bridge gaps from Phase 1 code review
